_comment: DECOMPRESSION finish line. Consumed by proposed `fulcrum scoreboard diff --dominance dominance-spec-decomp.json <artifact.json>`: loads ONE scoreboard-run artifact and checks each expanded cell's recorded verdict against `require`; emits PASS/FAIL/MISSING per cell + nonzero exit on any FAIL/MISSING. Decompression has ONE axis (wall); output bytes are the fixed decoded plaintext, so there is no level_class and no bytes axis here. The finish line = gzippy WIN-or-TIE vs the WHOLE field at every (box x corpus x T).
subject: gzippy-native

grid._expand: cells = for each box: box.threads x corpora, applying defaults (require, vs). A comparator whose threads_max==1 is only required to WIN/TIE in T1 cells; at T>1 it is compared as its T1-invariant reference (a user can always fall back to it) unless listed in per_cell overrides.

boxes.vs_note: arm64: rapidgzip gated OFF (ISA-L absent); vs = [libdeflate, zlibng, pigz] + gzip. igzip likely absent.

cells._comment: explicit override examples; the rest expand from grid. Per-arch vs overrides:

notes: The full expanded set = 5 corpora x (m1:3 + solvency:4 + neurotic:3 = 10 thread-cells) = 50 cells.
Each cell PASSES iff its recorded scoreboard verdict vs EVERY tool in vs is WIN or TIE (never LOSS/VOID/REFUSED).
This spec IS the decompression campaign's done-criterion, made checkable: when `diff --dominance` exits 0 against a frozen both-arch artifact, decompression is closed.
